Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Appointment Of Debra Dolch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Appointment Of Debra Dolch As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Continued To Jan-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sandra Zenteno in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that Ms. Ebey was not present. Ms. Zenteno stated that she was expecting Ms. Ebey to be here. The matter was passed to give Ms. Zenteno the opportunity to contact her Attorney. Upon returning and the matter being re-called, Ms. Zenteno stated that she was told by Ms. Ebey that she thought everything was taken care of, that she is filing the form today and that she is requesting a continuance. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement, etc. is continued to January 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Trustees Granting Power To Sell Real Property Or In The Alternative To Remove Vilma Ruiz As Co Trustee So That The Real Property Can Be Sold
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Petitioners
Lawrence Townsend for Vilma Ruiz (Respondent)

Counsel stated that they have not heard from Debra Dolch. Ms. Ebey stated that she was assured the messages were getting to Ms. Dolch, finds it odd that she hasn't heard back from her, the Co-Trustees are not in agreement so they agreed for Ms. Dolch to take over, and since Ms. Dolch has not signed the papers to accept as successor trustee she will call other fiduciaries. Mr. Townsend requested a 30 day continuance to get things wrapped up. The Petition for Instructions to Trustees Granting Power to Sell Real Property, etc. is Off Calendar. Parties can re-file when ready.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Trustees Granting Power To Sell Real Property Or In The Alternative To Remove Vilma Ruiz As Co Trustee So That The Real Property Can Be Sold
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Sandra Zenteno (Co-Trustee).
Lawrence Townsend Vilma Ruiz (Respondent).

Ms. Ebey stated that a petition to approve the settlement is prepared, that it was sent to Debra Dolch for signature and that she hopes to have it filed by next week. Mr. Townsend stated that the settlement is signed by everyone and that he does not see any reason as to why Ms. Dolch would not sign it. The Petition for Instructions to Trustees Granting Power to Sell Real Property, etc. is continued to June 25, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of the petition for approval of settlement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Elsy Tadeo; Lawrence Townsend for Vilma Ruiz Sousa; Robert Shepherd, Guardian ad Litem for Dora A. Aherne.

The Court noted that Mr. Shepherd had not filed a report, and requested his position regarding the approval of the settlement. Mr. Shepherd stated that he did approve of the settlement and agreed to the proposed waiver of bond. he added that he would be happy to provide the court with a written report if so desired. The Court asked if anyone else had any concerns regarding the approval of the settlement. Mr. Townsend responded that he did not. The Court stated that he did desire that Mr. Shepherd provide a written report, and Mr. Shepherd responded that he would have one on file within a few days. The Court noted that he had an original of the settlement agreement, and was advised by counsel that it was the only original. The Court asked if Mr. Shepherd was willing to add his signature to the settlement agreement. He did so. The Court granted the petition to approve settlement (subject to review of the order). The petition to remove Vilma Ruiz as successor trustee was ordered off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Petition For Removal Of Vilma M. Ruiz As Successor Trustee, Confirmation Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Jun-14-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with petitioner Gloria Coleman and for petitioner Elsy Tadeo; Sandra Zenteno; Lawrence Townsend for respondent Vilma Ruiz.

Mr. Townsend stated that the parties had reached a settlement of their dispute. The Court noted that the Trustor was not a signatory to the settlement agreement, and inquired whether her interests needed to be represented, either through a guardian ad litem or otherwise. Ms. Ebey stated that the trustor has severe Alzheimers and needs 24 hour care, and opined that she would not understand the settlement agreement. Mr. Townsend agreed that appointment of a guardian ad litem was one possible approach to the problem, but that the parties would like to explore finding a less expensive way of looking after the trustor's interests. The Court stated that the interests of the trustor must be taken into consideration. He added that there was an issue regarding the filing of a bond by the successor trustee, absent waivers of bond being filed by all the beneficiaries. In order to address the court's concerns, =the matter was continued to July 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

Petition For Removal Of Vilma M. Ruiz As Successor Trustee, Confirmation Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Continued To Mar-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Elsy Tadeo & Gloria Coleman. Lawrence Townsend for (respondent) Vilma Ruiz.

The Court stated that the parties had been sent to mediation but that there has been no status submitted. Mr. Townsend stated that they have not done mediation, that they have exchanged information, that they have a structure for settlement and that they can schedule mediation now. Ms. Ebey stated that they have shared all of the information, that there are some interpersonal issues and that they would like to see if mediation can help. The Court stated that it would have appreciated receiving a status and that it will need to sign a new order for mediation and signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 23, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 1, 2016. The petition for removal of Vilma Ruiz as successor trustee was continued to and the Parties are to return on March 8, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court expects the parties to obey the order and to report their status to the probate examiner.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Removal Of Vilma M. Ruiz As Successor Trustee, Confirmation Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Continued To Jan-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for (petitioner) Elsy Tadeo & Gloria Coleman who are present in court. Lawrence Townsend for (respondent) Vilma Ruiz who is present in court.

The Court stated that a petition has been filed to remove Vilma as successor trustee and confirm an alternate successor trustee, that it has been opposed, that if there is information that could be provided it should be and asked if she is open to working something out. Mr. Townsend stated that it has already been discussed, that information is being gathered and will be available soon and suggested having a mediation date set. Ms. Ebey stated that they have supplied documents and are gathering further documents, that they are working on a reverse mortgage and would like to mediate. The Court stated that it could order Pro Bono Mediation and will continue this matter out to give time to exchange information. Ms. Ebey stated that they need the reverse mortgage done soon as they are in dire circumstances. Mr. Townsend stated that it will need court approval as there is only one trustee and that the bank will want an order. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 15, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 21, 2016. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on January 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts . (Not Reported)
